title: Cannot delete tag on hub.docker.com {"errors":[{"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","message":"authentication required" ...]}
body:
Hi there,
Thanks for this awesome tool, very handy. I'm trying to delete a bunch of docker tags that are not useful anymore for a personal project (some automated built images from old branches) on docker hub. [More details here](https://gitlab.com/gableroux/unity3d/issues/10).

## What happens

When I try to delete an image or a tag from docker hub registry, I get the following error:

```json
FATA[0000] Failed to delete docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported: {"errors":[{"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","message":"authentication required","detail":[{"Type":"repository","Class":"","Name":"gableroux/unity3d","Action":"pull"}]}]}
 (401 Unauthorized)
```

## Steps to reproduce

```bash
export DOCKER_HUB_USER=gableroux
export DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD=`lpass show 'Dev/Docker' --password` # this returns the right password
export DOCKER_IMAGE=gableroux/unity3d
export DOCKER_TAG=2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported

skopeo --debug delete --creds=$DOCKER_HUB_USER:$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD docker://$DOCKER_IMAGE:$DOCKER_TAG
```
```
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ err <nil>
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/gableroux/unity3d/manifests/2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported
FATA[0000] Failed to delete docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported: {"errors":[{"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","message":"authentication required","detail":[{"Type":"repository","Class":"","Name":"gableroux/unity3d","Action":"pull"}]}]}
 (401 Unauthorized)
```

## What I tried so far

* I know my creds are ok (I can login online with these).
* I removed the special characters from my password and it's still failing
* I tried logging in directly to `registry-1.docker.io`
  ```bash
  docker login registry-1.docker.io
  ```
* I get a different error when my creds are not right

<details>
<summary>Wrong username example</summary>

```bash
export DOCKER_HUB_USER=SOME_WRONG_USERNAME
export DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD=`lpass show 'Dev/Docker' --password`
export DOCKER_IMAGE=gableroux/unity3d
export DOCKER_TAG=2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported

skopeo --debug delete --creds=$DOCKER_HUB_USER:$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD docker://$DOCKER_IMAGE:$DOCKER_TAG
```

```
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ err <nil>
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
FATA[0000] unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Wrong password example (same output)</summary>

```bash
export DOCKER_HUB_USER=gableroux
export DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD=SOME_WRONG_PASSWORD
export DOCKER_IMAGE=gableroux/unity3d
export DOCKER_TAG=2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported

skopeo --debug delete --creds=$DOCKER_HUB_USER:$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD docker://$DOCKER_IMAGE:$DOCKER_TAG
```

```
export DOCKER_HUB_USER=gableroux
export DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD=SOME_WRONG_PASSWORD
export DOCKER_IMAGE=gableroux/unity3d
export DOCKER_TAG=2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported

skopeo --debug delete --creds=$DOCKER_HUB_USER:$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD docker://$DOCKER_IMAGE:$DOCKER_TAG
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ err <nil>
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401
FATA[0000] unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password
```
</details>

So my creds are good. I tried `docker login`, but my `` looks like this (I removed a few lines):

```bash
cat $HOME/.docker/config.json | jq
```
```json
{
  "auths": {
    "https://index.docker.io": {},
    "https://index.docker.io/v1/": {},
    "index.docker.io": {},
    "registry-1.docker.io": {}
  },
  "HttpHeaders": {
    "User-Agent": "Docker-Client/18.06.0-ce (darwin)"
  },
  "credsStore": "osxkeychain"
}
```

The creds seem to be managed by `osxkeychain` (at least, it doesn't look like the example in the readme with hardcoded values).

## System and app informations

```bash
Darwin warbookpro-3.local 17.7.0 Darwin Kernel Version 17.7.0: Thu Jun 21 22:53:14 PDT 2018; root:xnu-4570.71.2~1/RELEASE_X86_64 x86_64
```
```bash
docker --version
Docker version 18.06.0-ce, build 0ffa825
```
```bash
skopeo --version
skopeo version 0.1.32-dev commit: e814f9605abe05a99b692225d458968a796d2843
```

comments.body:
Just curios, does 

```
skopeo --debug inspect --creds=$DOCKER_HUB_USER:$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD docker://$DOCKER_IMAGE:$DOCKER_TAG
```
work for you?

Thanks for your report.

The code was indeed not requesting sufficient permissions, and that should be fixed by https://github.com/containers/image/pull/487 .

Do note, though:
- That won’t make `skopeo delete` work on the Docker Hub:
  ```console
  $ skopeo --override-os linux delete docker://mtrmac/busybox-test:deletetag
  FATA[0002] Failed to delete /v2/mtrmac/busybox-test/manifests/sha256:5e8e0509e829bb8f990249135a36e81a3ecbe94294e7a185cc14616e5fad96bd: {"errors":[{"code":"UNSUPPORTED","message":"The operation is unsupported."}]}
   (405 Method Not Allowed) 
  ```

- `skopeo delete` is not an “untag” operation: it deletes _the image_, and _all_ tags referring to it within the repository. It is not possible to delete a single tag pointing at an image without deleting the image and all other tags pointing at it at the same time; the registry API does not provide such an operation.

@TomSweeneyRedHat yes inspect using creds works

<details>

<summary>inspect with creds command output</summary>

```bash
skopeo --debug inspect --creds=$DOCKER_HUB_USER:$DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD docker://$DOCKER_IMAGE:$DOCKER_TAG
```

```
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported

DEBU[0000] Downloading /v2/gableroux/unity3d/blobs/sha256:70edd55f52a80ba2d7fe999cc8c6a9c909ebfa75f8c744b6eafa448b83049c30
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/gableroux/unity3d/blobs/sha256:70edd55f52a80ba2d7fe999cc8c6a9c909ebfa75f8c744b6eafa448b83049c30
DEBU[0001] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0001]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/gableroux/unity3d:2017.1.0xb3Linux-9-fix-2017-target-was-unsupported

{
    "Name": "docker.io/gableroux/unity3d",
    "Digest": "sha256:04d757ddcf1e849fd65b5326c0a58c390e549a02e4f29b496772a6d69e3788a0",
    "RepoTags": [
[...]
```

</details>

---

@mtrmac thanks for the detailed answer. I wonder how I can do this then. What I'm trying to do is the same as the action on docker hub to remove the tag:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1264761/43839234-97062662-9aeb-11e8-9a00-ff85a42e9822.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1264761/43839247-9f8a615e-9aeb-11e8-9ac5-fc0481a1c57a.png)

But I have too many of them so I don't want to hit that button for my temporary images.

There is [this question on StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/30680703/remove-an-image-from-hub). I'm not really concerned about the size as I did not see any limits on docker hub (yet). I just don't want the tags to be accessible anymore as they should not be used.

>  I wonder how I can do this then. What I'm trying to do is the same as the action on docker hub to remove the tag:

Yes, I know this is available on the hub; nevertheless, there seems to be no (documented) API to do the same thing from a remote client. I’m afraid the web interface is the only option available.

Ok thanks. Since this is not possible, I’m closing this issue and I’ll write a js snippet or a selenium python script to do this using the ui instead. Thanks for your time and help, that’s really appreciated.
